title: Object-Oriented Programming
description: Hand-written closed questions made file by file from the OOP Markdown sources.

questions.prompt: Which topic belongs to an advanced OOP syllabus?
questions.explanation: The syllabus lists constructors and destructors among OOP topics.

questions.prompt: Why use a user-defined function such as display()?
choices: To organize repeated output logic into a reusable block
questions.explanation: Functions let code be reused and organized.

questions.prompt: What is a programming paradigm?
choices: A style or model for designing and organizing programs
questions.explanation: The lecture compares different ways to plan and build software.

questions.prompt: Why can different projects need different paradigms?
choices: Because project scale, risk, and structure can differ
questions.explanation: A small app and a safety-critical system need different design thinking.

questions.prompt: Why pass data through a member function instead of directly changing member data?
choices: To validate or filter data before storing it
To make data impossible to access
questions.explanation: The notes say member functions allow filtering before using member data.

questions.prompt: What is a class used for in OOP?
choices: Defining data and functions that describe a type of object
questions.explanation: A class groups state and behavior.

questions.prompt: What does a constructor do in a Counter class example?
choices: Initializes the object state such as count
questions.explanation: The example uses Counter() : count(0) to initialize count.

questions.prompt: What is function overloading?
choices: Defining functions with the same name but different parameter lists
Giving every function a random name
questions.explanation: Overloading lets one name handle related operations with different inputs.

questions.prompt: Why overload an operator for a user-defined type?
choices: To define what an operator means for objects of that class
questions.explanation: The compiler needs a definition for operators used with custom objects.

questions.prompt: Which operator pair is associated with stream insertion and extraction?
questions.explanation: The notes mention inserter and extractor operators.

questions.prompt: ما المقصود بتحميل المؤثرات في C++؟
choices: ربط مؤثر معين بدالة عضو في صنف حتى يعمل مع كائنات الصنف
questions.explanation: المحاضرة تشرح أن المؤثر يستدعي دالة عند تطبيقه على كائن.

questions.prompt: في مثال Pharmacy، لماذا يمكن استخدام ++item؟
choices: لتمثيل بيع عنصر وزيادة عداد المبيعات
questions.explanation: المثال يستخدم زيادة العداد عند بيع عنصر.

questions.prompt: What is inheritance in OOP?
choices: A subclass acquires properties of a parent class
questions.explanation: The notes define inheritance as acquiring parent class properties.

questions.prompt: Why is inheritance useful?
choices: It supports reusability and can improve reliability
questions.explanation: Inheritance helps reuse existing classes instead of recreating them.

questions.prompt: In public inheritance, what happens to public members of the base class?
choices: They remain public in the derived class
questions.explanation: The notes state public base members remain public under public inheritance.

questions.prompt: In private inheritance, what happens to public and protected base members?
choices: They become private in the derived class
questions.explanation: Private inheritance hides inherited interface from outside users.

questions.prompt: What does polymorphism mean?
questions.explanation: The lecture states polymorphism means many forms.

questions.prompt: What does a virtual function allow?
choices: Calling the derived class version through a base pointer or reference
questions.explanation: Virtual functions enable runtime dispatch to the correct overridden function.

questions.prompt: What is a friend function allowed to do?
choices: Access private or protected members of a class that declares it as friend
questions.explanation: Friend functions are external functions granted special access.

questions.prompt: Why might a Point class use a sum() member function?
choices: To compute a value from the object's axes or coordinates
questions.explanation: The lecture examples show summing point axes.

questions.prompt: What problem do templates solve?
choices: Writing generic code that works with different data types
questions.explanation: Templates avoid duplicating similar functions for each type.

questions.prompt: Why is a template better than writing many sum functions for int, float, and double?
choices: One generic definition can serve multiple types
questions.explanation: Templates parameterize the type.

questions.prompt: Which concept is fundamental when learning C++ for OOP?
questions.explanation: C++ OOP relies on classes and objects as core building blocks.

exams.title: Reference - C++ in One Hour a Day

questions.prompt: Why are constructors important in C++ classes?
choices: They initialize objects when they are created
They are used only for comments
questions.explanation: Constructors prepare object state.

questions.prompt: Which group contains core OOP ideas?
questions.explanation: These are standard OOP pillars used throughout C++ OOP references.

questions.prompt: في مشروع محول العملات، ما التحقق المطلوب؟
choices: التحقق من صحة القيم المدخلة وأنها رقمية
questions.explanation: وصف المشروع يطلب التأكد من صحة البيانات المدخلة.

questions.prompt: ما المطلوب من نظام إدارة سجلات بنكية؟
questions.explanation: مشاريع OOP تتطلب تمثيل كيانات وعمليات مناسبة.

questions.prompt: ما الفكرة الأساسية في البرمجة الشيئية؟
choices: تنظيم البرنامج حول كائنات تجمع البيانات والعمليات
كتابة كل شيء في دالة واحدة
questions.explanation: البرمجة الشيئية تقوم على الكائنات والأصناف وتجميع البيانات مع السلوك.

questions.prompt: لماذا تصلح مشاريع مثل ATM أو Bank Account لتطبيق OOP؟
choices: لأنها تحتوي كيانات وبيانات وعمليات يمكن تمثيلها بأصناف وكائنات
لأنها لا تحتاج إلى أي بيانات
questions.explanation: مشاريع OOP الجيدة تحتوي كائنات واضحة مثل حساب أو بطاقة أو طالب.
